name: Indium phosphide (InP)
description: III-V semiconductor used for EMLs, DFB lasers, and photodiodes in 800G/1.6T pluggable transceivers and the InP die in co-packaged optics. The 4" to 6" wafer transition is the binding constraint for the 2026 to 2028 optics ramp.
stackPosition: InP wafer → epitaxy (MOCVD) → device fab (EML, DFB, PD) → pluggable transceiver OR co-packaged optics → switch / GPU rack → datacenter network spine. The wafer layer is the chokepoint; downstream device capacity at Coherent and Lumentum can scale faster than upstream wafer supply.

companies.name: Ayar Labs
companies.note: TeraPHY optical I/O chiplet; ELS (external laser source) architecture for CPO
companies.funding: Series D · $155M total · 2024 expansion led by AMD Ventures

companies.note: Passage photonic interconnect substrate; competes with CPO + electrical fabric simultaneously

companies.name: Celestial AI
companies.note: Photonic Fabric — chip-to-chip + chip-to-memory optical bandwidth
companies.investors: US Innovative Technology Fund, Temasek, Porsche, Imec

companies.name: Nubis Communications
companies.note: Linear-drive pluggable optics + optical I/O chiplets for AI fabrics

companies.name: NcodiN
companies.note: III-V-on-Si nanolasers; substrate-bypass thesis on the 6" wafer constraint

insight: The chokepoint is not InP devices — it's 6" wafer qualification at Sumitomo and JX. Anyone with a credible path to 6" epi yield in 2026 owns the next-cycle margin pool. Watch for non-Japanese supply emerging in Korea or via CHIPS-funded US pilot lines. The investable wedge is the qualification race itself, not the incumbents. Contrarian path: substrate-bypass plays like III-V-on-silicon that route around the wafer constraint entirely.

debates.question: Does Sumitomo or JX reach production-yield 6" InP wafers by end of 2026?
debates.currentBelief: Sumitomo more likely than JX; both still pilot as of mid-2026. Updated after recent OFC commentary

debates.question: Does CPO meaningfully cannibalize pluggable transceiver volumes by 2027?
debates.currentBelief: CPO ramps but pluggables grow alongside through 2028
